figures.caption: Figure 6. Roofline model analysis of π0 computational characteristics. All three hardware platforms (RTX 4090, AGX Orin, Jetson Thor) test the GemmaDecoderlayer layer in the Action Expert (marked with asterisk). Additionally, RTX 4090 tests the VLM backbone, with separate profiling of GemmaMLP and GemmaAttention components.

Figure 7. Top: Analysis of relative L1 differences (L1rel) for key model components (model output, timestep embedding, noisy input, global feature, and mid-module blocks) between consecutive diffusion steps. Bottom: Schematic illustration of the DP-Cache mechanism. During the stable diffusion segment, computed results (green) are cached and broadcast to subsequent steps (gray).
Table 11. DP-Cache: inference latency and task capability (success rate, SR) of π0 on an RTX 4090.
Table 10. Hardware specifications for the GPU, XPU, and NPU platforms used in our evaluation.
Figure 12. Real-world deployment: task execution stages with time and energy consumption analysis across hardware platforms on a Franka arm using π0.5.
Figure 8. Visual demonstration of the manipulation task on Franka using π0.5-droid: Cleaning up the table task.

Figure 2. VLA model performance across hardware accelerators. The bar chart displays inference frequencies (log scale) across hardware platforms for VLA models. The pink line represents capability scores on LIBERO (Liu et al., 2023). Horizontal lines indicate frequency thresholds (1Hz-30Hz).
Figure 11. Visual demonstration of two manipulation tasks on the JAKA S5 dual-arm robot using fine-tuned π0.

Figure 4. Hardware selection guidelines under multi-dimensional metrics (CET). The bubble size encodes hardware cost (Cost), the x-axis represents energy consumption (Energy), and the y-axis represents normalized inference latency (Time).
Figure 5. Profiling Results of π0 on NVIDIA platforms. The blue, green, and red numbers on the profiling trace of each hardware correspond to the SM utilization of the same color phase.
Figure 3. Energy-Latency Analysis: Energy Consumption and Latency Breakdown across Models and Hardware Platforms.
